mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects strong consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical level, it indicates a tight labor market that can increase wage pressures, compressing corporate profit margins and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 3.5% and S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating inflation and robust industrial production indicates an overheating economy, which typically forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized change > 5%
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and INDPRO triggers are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, a stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains stable and CPIAUCSL growth is below 2% annually, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts reflect strong consumer and business confidence in future economic conditions, acting as a leading indicator for sustained economic growth and subsequently driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently remains above 1500 but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in over 40% of such periods over a 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A substantial increase in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust and expanding labor market, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently shows 3-month average changes above 150k, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid contraction in industrial production indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing and broader economic activity, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% in a month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of such occurrences over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating GDP growth and persistent inflation signals stagflationary conditions, which erode corporate profitability and increase the equity risk premium, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% simultaneously, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive.
